title: Trustworthy Research Assistant With Verified Citations
description: An improved RAG architecture that grounds answers in uploaded papers, verifies citations, and routes important outputs through review.
user_idea: Build a trustworthy research assistant that can read papers, answer questions, and provide verified citations.
assumptions: Uploaded papers may contain private notes or unpublished work.
Answers must cite retrieved paper passages.
Human review is required before high-trust external use.

naive_baseline.summary: User question goes directly to one LLM, which returns an answer without retrieval, source trace, citation checks, or review.
failure_modes: Invented citations or unsupported claims can reach the user.
Private uploaded material has no retention or deletion policy.
There is no reviewer for important research outputs.

nodes.name: Document Upload
nodes.description: Collects papers or notes that the assistant is allowed to use.

risks.description: Uploaded papers may include private or unpublished information.
risks.mitigation: Show retention assumptions and avoid storing documents longer than needed.

cost_estimate.notes: No model cost at upload time.

latency_estimate.notes: Upload latency depends on document size.

alternatives.name: Paste abstract only
alternatives.tradeoff: Lower privacy exposure but weaker grounding.
alternatives.when_to_use: Use when the full paper cannot be uploaded.

nodes.explanation_for_beginner: This is the door where trusted source material enters the system.

nodes.name: Text Extraction
nodes.description: Converts uploaded files into plain text and metadata.

outputs.name: document_text
outputs.description: Extracted text with page or section metadata.

risks.description: Poor extraction can lose tables, formulas, or citation context.
risks.mitigation: Keep page metadata and expose extraction uncertainty.

latency_estimate.notes: Large PDFs may take several seconds.

alternatives.name: Manual text paste
alternatives.tradeoff: More effort for the user but fewer parsing errors.

nodes.explanation_for_beginner: The system cannot search PDFs well until their words are extracted.

nodes.name: Chunking
nodes.description: Splits long document text into smaller searchable sections.

risks.description: Chunks can split claims from their supporting evidence.
risks.mitigation: Use overlap and keep paper, page, and section metadata.

alternatives.name: Section-aware chunking
alternatives.tradeoff: Better context but more implementation effort.

nodes.explanation_for_beginner: Chunking turns large papers into bite-sized pieces that retrieval can find.

nodes.name: Embedding
nodes.description: Creates vector representations for each chunk.

risks.description: Embedding many documents can create repeated cost.
risks.mitigation: Cache embeddings and avoid recomputing unchanged documents.

cost_estimate.notes: Embedding cost grows with document volume.

alternatives.name: Keyword index
alternatives.tradeoff: Cheaper and simpler, but misses semantic matches.

nodes.explanation_for_beginner: Embeddings make similar ideas close together so the system can retrieve relevant passages.

nodes.name: Knowledge Base
nodes.description: Stores chunk text, vectors, source metadata, and retention settings.

config.retention_policy: delete on project reset or user request

risks.description: Private uploads need clear storage and deletion expectations.
risks.mitigation: Expose retention policy and deletion control in the implementation plan.

cost_estimate.notes: Storage cost is modest for a demo corpus.

latency_estimate.notes: Index lookup should be fast after ingestion.

alternatives.name: Session-only index
alternatives.tradeoff: Better privacy but users must re-upload documents.

nodes.explanation_for_beginner: The knowledge base is the assistant's approved library.

nodes.name: User Question
nodes.description: Receives the research question to answer.

outputs.name: question
outputs.description: The user's question about the uploaded papers.

risks.description: Ambiguous questions can produce broad or unsupported answers.
risks.mitigation: Ask for clarification when the retrieval query is too vague.

cost_estimate.notes: No model cost at input time.

alternatives.name: Guided question form
alternatives.tradeoff: More structure but less flexible input.

nodes.explanation_for_beginner: This captures what the user wants to learn from the papers.

nodes.name: Retrieval
nodes.description: Finds the most relevant document chunks for the question.

config.fallback_when_empty: ask for more documents or answer with uncertainty

risks.description: Retrieval may miss a key passage and make the answer incomplete.
risks.mitigation: Show retrieved sources and allow uncertainty when evidence is weak.

cost_estimate.notes: Vector lookup is cheaper than generation.

latency_estimate.notes: Expected to run quickly after indexing.

alternatives.name: Hybrid search
alternatives.tradeoff: Better recall but more indexing complexity.

nodes.explanation_for_beginner: Retrieval chooses which parts of the papers the LLM should see before answering.

nodes.name: Grounded Prompt
nodes.description: Builds instructions that require answers to use retrieved sources and state uncertainty.

outputs.name: answer_prompt
outputs.description: Instruction bundle for the answer model.

risks.description: Weak instructions may allow unsupported claims.
risks.mitigation: Explicitly require citations and uncertainty statements.

cost_estimate.notes: Prompt assembly itself has no model cost.

nodes.explanation_for_beginner: The prompt tells the LLM the rules for using evidence.

nodes.name: LLM Answer Generator
nodes.description: Drafts an answer using only the retrieved context and prompt instructions.

risks.description: The model can still invent claims or citations.
risks.mitigation: Send every draft to citation verification before final output.

cost_estimate.notes: Generation cost depends on context and answer length.

latency_estimate.notes: Answer generation usually dominates user wait time.

nodes.explanation_for_beginner: This is the writing step, but it is boxed in by retrieved evidence.

nodes.name: Citation Verifier
nodes.description: Checks that each cited claim is supported by retrieved passages.

outputs.name: verified_draft
outputs.description: Draft with verification notes and unsupported claims marked.

risks.description: The verifier may miss subtle citation problems.
risks.mitigation: Route important outputs to human review and show verification notes.

cost_estimate.notes: Verification may use another model call.

latency_estimate.notes: Adds latency but catches high-value failures.

alternatives.name: Rule-based citation presence check
alternatives.tradeoff: Cheaper but cannot prove claim support.

nodes.explanation_for_beginner: The verifier is a second checkpoint that asks whether the answer really matches the sources.

nodes.name: Human Review
nodes.description: Lets a user review verification notes before trusting the answer.

inputs.name: verified_draft
inputs.description: Verified answer draft with any warnings.

outputs.name: approved_answer
outputs.description: Reviewed answer ready for final display.

risks.description: Reviewers may approve too quickly when there are many warnings.
risks.mitigation: Highlight only the highest-impact verification failures.

cost_estimate.notes: Human time cost, not model cost.

latency_estimate.notes: Human review adds variable waiting time.

alternatives.name: User approval only for flagged drafts
alternatives.tradeoff: Faster routine answers but less oversight.

nodes.explanation_for_beginner: A person gets the final say when the answer may be important.

nodes.name: Final Answer
nodes.description: Displays the reviewed answer, citations, uncertainty notes, and source list.

config.output_format: answer, citations, uncertainty notes, source list

risks.description: Users may treat a polished answer as guaranteed correct.
risks.mitigation: Show verification status and a clear uncertainty note.

cost_estimate.notes: Display step has no model cost.

alternatives.name: Annotated answer
alternatives.tradeoff: More transparent but longer to read.

nodes.explanation_for_beginner: This is what the user sees after the safeguards have run.
